statement: The continuum trace-frame lower-bound theorem supplies the model-specific trace-frame inputs needed for high-block Mosco projection convergence: a positive continuum trace-frame lower bound, quadrature consistency, and a bounded sampled correction right inverse.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from the continuum trace-frame theorem.

traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.label: trace quadrature consistency
traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.reason: Imported from the continuum trace-frame theorem.

boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.label: bounded sampled-trace correction
boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.reason: Imported from the continuum trace-frame theorem.

traceFrameMoscoInputConsequenceStatus.label: trace-frame Mosco input consequence
traceFrameMoscoInputConsequenceStatus.reason: Together these three facts give the model-specific high-block recovery and closed-trace compatibility input.

proof: Import the continuum trace-frame lower bound.
Import trace quadrature consistency on the active block.
Import the bounded sampled-trace correction right inverse.
Expose only these three inputs to the high-block Mosco projection theorem.
